title: Infoton Virtual Mitochondria — snapshot contract
description: Self-describing output of the Quantum Heartbeat model (Walker 2026, Januarian Physics 1, 1.3). Single independent input is membrane potential Δψm; every other field is a deterministic function of it. Field groups encode provenance: paper_chain is first-principles from the paper; platform_extension are infoton.ai heuristics; exploratory fields appear in neither source and are surfaced for review.

model_version.description: Semantic version of the model. Bump major on any change to the equation chain or constants.

t_bath_k.description: Thermal bath temperature used in the Wien/ΔT step.

psi_mmV.description: Mitochondrial membrane potential Δψm — the sole independent variable (e.g. from a JC-1 assay).

paper_chain.description: First-principles chain from Walker (2026), §1–§4. Each field is exact given Δψm and the constants.

delta_t_k.description: ΔT = T − T_bath. May be ≤ 0 below the coherence floor.

platform_extension.description: infoton.ai platform heuristics derived from the coherence ratio τ/τ_healthy. Not first-principles; use with that caveat.

tau_coh_ps.description: Coherence time τ = ℏ/(k_B·ΔT); 0 when ΔT ≤ 0.

exploratory.description: Candidate fission/fusion mappings present in NEITHER the paper nor infoton.ai. Two models are surfaced because they disagree at the hyperpolarized end — an open question flagged for review. F/F > 1 = fission-dominant, < 1 = fusion-dominant.

fission_fusion_coherence_model.description: Model A: fission rises as coherence (τ/τ_healthy) is lost.

fission_fusion_psi_model.description: Model B: F/F ≈ 2^((150−Δψm)/30), depolarization-driven fission per Twig 2008 / Youle 2012.

coherence_model_predicts.description: Direction predicted by Model A at this Δψm.

psi_model_predicts.description: Direction predicted by Model B at this Δψm.

models_disagree.description: True when the two models predict opposite directions — the case to flag for review.

review_note.description: Standing caveat: the F/F axis is exploratory and the models conflict at hyperpolarization.
